info.topictitle: The Successful Spread of Lutheran Ideas

markdown:
## **Reasons for the Successful Spread of Ideas**


1. The **printing press **played a huge role in spreading Luther’s ideas quickly.
2. Luther’s message was **not just for scholars or priests.** Ordinary townspeople could read and share it too.

![A 16th-century printing press was the “social media” of Luther’s day.](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/54d19db39c5cd30864e5fc5c767fe2a06acd1917-1200x630.jpg)

## **Frederick the Wise**


1. **Frederick the Wise**, Elector of Saxony, **gave Luther protection **when the Church tried to silence him.
2. **Arranged for Luther to be hidden in Wartburg Castle,** where he translated the New Testament into German.

![Without Frederick’s support, Luther might not have survived long enough for his ideas to take root.](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/8f2bff6d1d2d83dbcadf0ddd5918ed0a0f12b41a-3000x4820.jpg)

## **Attitudes**


1. Many German **princes supported Luther **because his ideas gave them more independence from the pope and the Holy Roman Emperor.
2. **Some also saw **a chance to take over Church lands and wealth.
3. In the cities, Luther’s **call for reform appealed to ordinary people and civic leaders **who wanted more control over local religious life.

## **The Imperial Diets of Worms (1521) and Speyer (1526 and 1529)**


1. At the Diet of Worms in 1521,** Luther was told to take back his ideas but refused**. 
2. The** Diet of Speyer in 1526 gave princes temporary freedom **to decide whether to enforce the Edict of Worms. This allowed Lutheranism to grow stronger.
3. At the **second Diet of Speyer in 1529**, Catholic leaders tried to reverse this decision. In protest, the Lutheran princes stood up for their beliefs, and **this is where the word “Protestant” first came from.**

![Luther was declared an outlaw, yet his message kept spreading.](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/b993f69d4e8a5bd9ec24129850892fa3bbe70931-1292x810.jpg)

<callout type="warning">* Students sometimes confuse Frederick the Wise with other princes or think he was motivated only by faith. His main motive was **political protection of his territory.**
* Students sometimes just say “printing was important” without explaining _why_ (reach, speed, access to ordinary people).</callout>

<callout type="exam_technique">* Always connect the **printing press** to the idea of _mass communication_ and the rapid spread of reform.
* Show _both_ political (princes) and popular (cities/people) support to cover breadth.</callout>

<callout type="self_review">* Examine the role of** political protection and princely support **in the survival of Lutheranism between 1521 and 1529.
* To what extent did popular support in towns and cities contribute to the success of the **early Reformation?**</callout>
